content.full_text:
ALAN W. COURTNEY
A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION
LAWYER
567 SAN NICOLAS DRIVE
SUITE 408
NEWPORT BEACH, CALIFORNIA 92660
(714) 760-1994
FAX (714) 760-2820

January 29, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Re: FRIENDS OF GEORGE HANNA; OPINION REQUEST

Dear Sirs:

I represent George Hanna and Friends of George Hanna, a controlled committee. The following facts are presented to you in order to obtain an opinion regarding the efficacy of a settlement agreement entered into with Arnold Steinberg and Associates, Inc. ("Steinberg").

George Hanna was a candidate for mayor of Santa Ana in the November, 1988, general election. In September and October of 1988, the Friends of George Hanna committee obtained two public opinion polls from Steinberg. Steinberg billed the committee $9,500 for these polls.

The committee became insolvent and lacked sufficient funds to pay for these polls after George Hanna lost the election. On August 7, 1989, Steinberg filed a Municipal Court lawsuit against George Hanna to collect the $9,500, plus interest, costs and attorney's fees. The committee was not named as a defendant in the lawsuit.

George Hanna answered the complaint by generally denying the allegations and asserted various affirmative defenses. The case was set for trial on January 22, 1989, at which time the following settlement agreement was reached between George Hanna and Steinberg in open court:

1. The committee agreed to conduct a fund raising effort within 90 days.

2. George Hanna personally guaranteed that a minimum of $2,000 would be paid to Steinberg if less than $2,000 is raised.

F.P.P.C.
January 29, 1990
Page Two


3.    If more than $2,000 is raised, Steinberg is to
receive all of the money raised, up to a maximum of $11,500.
Anything over $11,500 would be kept by the committee to retire
other debts from the election.

4.    The committee's former campaign consultant, Frank
Caternichio (who originally ordered the surveys, who was present in
court, and consented to this arrangement), will supply the
committee with a mailing list of contributors and assist in the
fund raising effort.  For this, Mr. Caternichio will be paid a
commission of 5% of all sums raised in excess of $11,500.

5.    George Hanna will personally pay for all mailing
costs associated with the fund raising effort as the committee has
no money at this time.

6.    All funds would be raised in the name of the
committee and will be deposited into its checking account.

7.    Upon payment to Steinberg under the terms of this
settlement, the lawsuit would be dismissed with prejudice.

     This letter is sent because the Judge hearing the case
had concerns as to whether this settlement complied with the
Political Reform Act.  I represented to the Court that it did and
am writing this letter to obtain a formal opinion from the
Commission so this settlement may go forward without fear of
violating the Act.

     As time is of the essence to this settlement agreement,
I would appreciate a prompt response to this opinion request.  If
you need any additional facts or information, please do not
hesitate to call.

                                   Very truly yours,

                                   ALAN W. COURTNEY
                                   A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

                                   [signature]
                                   Alan W. Courtney

cc:  Mr. Hanna
     Mr. Steinberg

Box 807 Sacramento, CA 95804 Re: FRIENDS OF GEORGE HANNA; OPINION REQUEST Dear Sirs: I represent George Hanna and Friends of George Hanna, a controlled committee. The following facts are presented to you in order to obtain an opinion regarding the efficacy of a settlement agreement entered into with Arnold Steinberg and Associates, Inc. ("Steinberg"). George Hanna was a candidate for mayor of Santa Ana in the November, 1988, general election. In September and October of 1988, the Friends of George Hanna committee obtained two public opinion polls from Steinberg. Steinberg billed the committee $9,500 for these polls. The committee became insolvent and lacked sufficient funds to pay for these polls after George Hanna lost the election. On August 7, 1989, Steinberg filed a Municipal Court lawsuit against George Hanna to collect the $9,500, plus interest, costs and attorney's fees. The committee was not named as a defendant in the lawsuit. George Hanna answered the complaint by generally denying the allegations and asserted various affirmative defenses. The case was set for trial on January 22, 1989, at which time the following settlement agreement was reached between George Hanna and Steinberg in open court: 1. The committee agreed to conduct a fund raising effort within 90 days. 2. George Hanna personally guaranteed that a minimum of $2,000 would be paid to Steinberg if less than $2,000 is raised. 3. If more than $2,000 is raised, Steinberg is to receive all of the money raised, up to a maximum of $11,500. Anything over $11,500 would be kept by the committee to retire other debts from the election. 4. The committee's former campaign consultant, Frank Caternichio (who originally ordered the surveys, who was present in court, and consented to this arrangement), will supply the committee with a mailing list of contributors and assist in the fund raising effort. For this, Mr. Caternichio will be paid a commission of 5% of all sums raised in excess of $11,500. 5. George Hanna will personally pay for all mailing costs associated with the fund raising effort as the committee has no money at this time. 6. All funds would be raised in the name of the committee and will be deposited into its checking account. 7. Upon payment to Steinberg under the terms of this settlement, the lawsuit would be dismissed with prejudice. This letter is sent because the Judge hearing the case had concerns as to whether this settlement complied with the Political Reform Act. I represented to the Court that it did and I am writing this letter to obtain a formal opinion from the Commission so this settlement may go forward without fear of violating the Act. As time is of the essence to this settlement agreement, I would appreciate a prompt response to this opinion request. If you need any additional facts or information, please do not hesitate to call. Very truly yours, ALAN W. COURTNEY A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION Alan W. Courtney cc: Mr.
